I'll index this project using the codebase-memory tooling — first checking how indexing is set up and which MCP tools are available.

[REDACTED]

Checking whether the project is already indexed, then starting a full index.

[REDACTED]

Indexing finished. Gathering a quick architecture summary.

[REDACTED]

The project is indexed and ready to use.

## Index summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Project name** | `D-Elay_Backend-master` |
| **Status** | Ready |
| **Nodes** | 9,924 |
| **Edges** | 22,479 |
| **Mode** | Full (with LSP call/usage resolution) |

## What was captured

- **Languages:** 1,196 C# files (+ 4 JS, 2 CSS)
- **Packages:** Admin & Customer bounded contexts — `Core`, `Infrastructure`, `Api`, `UI`, `Share`, `SharedKernel`
- **Routes:** 63 API routes (including new Chat endpoints like `/chat/conversations`)
- **Layers:** Clean Architecture — `Share`/`SharedKernel` as core, `Api`/`UI` as entry points, `Core`/`Infrastructure` as internal

## Hotspots (most referenced symbols)

- `Result.Match`, `Error.ToString`, `SaveChangesAsync`, `Result.Success`
- `CurrentUserService.UserId`, `BaseViewModel.Saved`/`Saving`

## Persistent artifact

A shareable index was written to `.codebase-memory/graph.db.zst`. Teammates can bootstrap from this instead of re-indexing. You can commit it if you want the team to share the same graph.

## What you can ask next

With the knowledge graph active, you can ask things like:

- "Who calls `SendChatMessageCommandHandler`?"
- "Trace the call chain for message creation"
- "Show dead code in Customer.Application"
- "What are the dependencies of the Chat module?"

Re-index after large structural changes with: `index_repository(repo_path="d:\Elay_Backend-master")`.
